body.value: 士族人員 貳萬三千八百二十三人
士族人員 (しぞくじんいん) 二万三千八百二十三人

Shizoku persons = 23,823. Saga's distinctively large shizoku class — about 30% of total population — reflects the domain's policy of preserving samurai status broadly across the late-bakumatsu reform period. Saga's 大隈 Ōkuma Shigenobu rose from this samurai milieu to become a central-government leader.
卒人員 四萬三千四百八拾八人
卒人員 (そつじんいん) 四万三千四百八十八人

Sotsu persons = 43,488. Saga's sotsu count is nearly double its shizoku count — unusual proportion. Combined shizoku+sotsu = 67,311, giving a retainer-per-1000-koku ratio of 92.9 against the 724,739 godaka.
卒戸數 九千三百六十九軒
卒戸数 (そつこすう) 九千三百六十九軒
Sotsu households = 9,369. Cross-check: 43,488 persons / 9,369 households = 4.64 persons per household. Consistent.
○佐倉藩 (page bottom — NEW DOMAIN BEGINS HERE)
佐倉藩 (さくらはん)。下総国の堀田・酒井家。佐嘉と紛らわしいが別藩。

Sakura-han marker — this page is STACKED. Saga's entry ENDS at mid-page, and ○佐倉藩 (Sakura-han, a different Sakai-family domain in Shimousa Province) BEGINS at the bottom-left. The two domains share a page but are entirely separate entities. The page-bottom annotation 藩制一覧表第六 (佐倉) corresponds to the Sakura entry. This stacked-page pattern is the basis of HI's cross-page contamination risk — values can drift between adjacent entries if extraction isn't careful.
